page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 architecture instance belonging to the E2 server family. It is equipped with 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor, utilizing simultaneous multithreading with 2 threads per core. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the e2-highcpu-32 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2690.17 and a PassMark CPU encryption test score of 34292.03, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core and memory performance metrics generally fall within the average range. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core score of 43150.61 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11360.0. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block cached speed of 1819.06 GB/sec and an uncached large block read speed of 82.87 GB/sec. Database operations on PassMark scored 12925.17, while Redis SET operations reached 30.82 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the e2-highcpu-32 is its highly constrained memory allocation of 1.0 GB per vCPU, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications or large-scale in-memory databases. However, this resource density provides qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The instance is suited for tasks such as software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight large language model inference using small models, where its strong single-core performance and dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs can be leveraged effectively.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance from the E2 family, featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor. It is configured with 32.0 GB of memory, resulting in a low 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and contains no bundled local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and PassMark encryption scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 11360.0. Due to its low memory-to-CPU ratio, the instance presents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-heavy databases but highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads. It is suited for software compilation, static web serving, and text processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 instance with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance for compute-bound workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-32 features 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A compute-focused x86_64 instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a lean memory profile optimized for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a lean 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities in the top 25%.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, prioritizing pure compute resources.
Suited for software compilation, text processing, and static web serving.
